intentional_test_features: Exertional syncope is the pivotal red flag: in pulmonary hypertension, syncope/presyncope on exertion means the right ventricle can no longer augment output against high pulmonary vascular resistance - a marker of high sudden-death risk that must not be under-weighted
Right-sided congestion cluster: RV heave, loud P2, right-sided S3, markedly raised JVP with a prominent 'a' wave, tender hepatomegaly and peripheral oedema indicate right ventricular failure

Left- vs right-sided distinction: the ABSENCE of orthopnoea/PND/bibasal crackles with predominantly right-sided signs points to pulmonary hypertension/right heart failure rather than left-sided heart failure
Resting hypoxaemia (SpO2 92% on room air) with progressive exertional dyspnoea signals significant disease and limited reserve

Strict activity restriction and urgent assessment: exertion can precipitate syncope/sudden death, so the AI Doctor must mandate rest and arrange urgent echocardiography/specialist evaluation - not a routine referral

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
